files.00_Overview.md: This is a quick-reference overview file. Convert to structured overview with: 1) Clear Learning Objectives section at start, 2) Prerequisites box listing required math/physics background, 3) Skills checklist (What You Will Learn), 4) Key Takeaways summary box at end, 5) Fix broken mermaid flowchart (references non-existent 01_RANS_Models.md - should be 01_Turbulence_Fundamentals.md, 02_Advanced_Turbulence.md doesn't exist)
files.01_Turbulence_Fundamentals.md: Add standard structure: 1) Learning Objectives at top, 2) Skills checklist for this topic, 3) Prerequisites section if needed, 4) Key Takeaways box at end. The content quality is good - comprehensive physics coverage. Keep the dual-language (Thai/English) approach. Fix broken Related Documents link to 02_Advanced_Turbulence.md
files.02_RANS_Models.md: Add standard structure: 1) Learning Objectives, 2) Skills checklist, 3) Key Takeaways. Content is well-structured with good model comparison tables. Consider adding 'What' (model definitions), 'Why' (selection criteria), 'How' (OpenFOAM setup) labels to sections. Fix broken Related Documents link to 02_Advanced_Turbulence.md
files.03_Wall_Treatment.md: Add standard structure: 1) Learning Objectives, 2) Skills checklist, 3) Key Takeaways. Content is excellent with practical troubleshooting. Fix broken Related Documents link to 02_Advanced_Turbulence.md. Consider moving decision flowchart to Learning Objectives section
files.04_LES_Fundamentals.md: Add standard structure: 1) Learning Objectives, 2) Skills checklist, 3) Key Takeaways. Content is comprehensive. Fix broken Related Documents link to 05_Validation_Best_Practices.md (doesn't exist). The SGS model comparison could include a 'Recommended for' column

global_strategy: Apply 3W Framework (What/Why/How) consistently: 'What' = definitions and equations, 'Why' = physical meaning and selection criteria, 'How' = OpenFOAM implementation. All files need Learning Objectives at start and Key Takeaways at end. Fix broken internal links in Related Documents sections. Consider adding a skills progression tracker across the turbulence modeling sequence. The dual-language (Thai/English) approach is effective and should be maintained.
